summary: Brits who want to save money on a trip to Walt Disney World in Orlando, Florida, should consider what day and time they book – there's a cheapest month and you can get a free dining plan

paragraphs: We Brits love a good theme park holiday and for many a trip to Walt Disney World in Florida would be a dream come true – but it can cost a hefty sum.
Luckily, there are a number of hacks that can save you a fortune from the best day to book flights to how to get free food for your entire holiday.

Booking insider Tessa Bishop, Head of Sales and Customer Services at Ocean Florida , shared the best times you should look at booking your dream Disney holiday to make sure you get the cheapest deal for what your family needs. That way you can get your dream holiday at the right budget.

According to Tessa the best time to book a Disney holiday is usually through April and May (for the following year) as this is when they usually run their early booking offers. That means that if you're thinking about a Disney trip, now couldn’t be a better time to book. Many of the deals are currently live and due to limited availability sell out fast so you may need to be quick.

One such offer is Free Disney Dining and Drinks, which launched on the April 18 – it is the perfect opportunity to grab a deal that can save you thousands on your holiday as you don't have to shell out a fortune for food in the parks. Tessa explained: "For the first time since 2019, they have brought back the Disney Dine offer which can save up to £2,000 per booking. With this package, you can enjoy free dining and drinks during your 2025 trip when staying in Disney Hotels."

The offers are just one way to save, however, as even something as simple as the day of the week you book can make a difference to the cost of your holiday. Tessa noted: "Always try to make a booking on a Tuesday, Wednesday or Sunday as this is likely where you can get a better price on flights."
According to the expert, the best month to book is January as this is when most flight sales are on by all major airlines, which Tessa says: "can save you up to £1,200 per booking."

Tessa isn't the only travel insider with useful tips on saving money though. Marketing Manager at Ocean Florida Abby Dunn, who has travelled to Florida over 42 times, shared her top six savvy booking tips to cut the cost of your holiday.

Abby said: "I’d always recommend purchasing your park tickets at the time of booking your package holiday. The main theme parks increase their prices often, so securing them as early as you can means you have that lower price locked in and can avoid any fluctuations - which could result in you spending more."

The Florida fan explained: "Try and be flexible with your departure date, and if you can, don’t restrict yourself to the usual seven, ten, or fourteen-night holidays. Often odd durations like nine or fifteen nights can be cheaper, even once you have considered extra hotel costs and spending money."

She noted: "For Orlando Holidays you’re likely to look at flights straight to Orlando, but I would suggest looking at alternative airports nearby such as Tampa as doing this can save you at least £400 per booking depending on the time of year you travel and still provide well-connected transfer facilities ."

"We already know to try and avoid UK school holidays, but also consider the same for students in the USA, as this also has an impact on accommodation prices and crowd levels in the parks," commented Abby. "For example, UK children have two weeks off at Easter. In the US, however, this is classed as ‘Spring Break’ and thanks to the different states having different school out dates, this busy holiday period often lasts a month!"

The expert said: "Disney World is a great event space and because of this, they tend to host a wide variety of sporting events. Check the calendars to try to avoid any events of this type, such as a RunDisney Event and the Cheerleading & Dance World Championships, as this will increase costs significantly.
"Of course, if you’re looking to tie in your trip with the Fifa World Cup, it’s important to start thinking about booking your 2026 Orlando Holiday now to avoid staggering prices!"

Abby commented: "I would always consider a multi-centre stay to save money. By staying at Disney for four nights for example and then twinning this with a villa stay, you often save money on accommodation but still get the benefits of onsite stays. There are so many amazing villas available at different price points that cater to different needs, making them the perfect place to stay during the duration of your Florida holiday."
If you’re looking for Florida Holidays in 2025/2026 , enquire with Ocean Florida today.

summary: WARNING GRAPHIC DETAIL: Brave Marina Lacerda has described the horrific abuse she suffered at the hands of the billionaire paedophile and says he treated his victims like dolls

paragraphs: A woman who was raped from the ages of 14 to 17 by Jeffrey Epstein has revealed she was never allowed into the paedophile financier’s bedroom during the horrific years of her abuse.
Marina Lacerda, identified as "Minor Victim 1" in the Epstein files, says the predator treated his victims like dolls and even asked to meet her sister – who was 11-years-old.

The 37-year-old said the horrific abuse took place in the massage room of Epstein’s $77million Manhattan mansion as his bedroom was off limits. But there was one occasion where she was allowed into the monster's private quarters at around age 16 or 17, and he was not the only adult in the room.

Lacerda’s recollection of the ordeal is patchy due to having “blacked out” parts of the abuse over time. But she remembered the sex trafficker had a massive bedframe in his room with soft, white, cotton sheets. The victim added she was lured into the room with another 18-year-old girl – a Brazilian model – who Lacerda said Epstein “was in love with.”

Lacerda said Epstein told her: "We're going to have a special day today. We're going to have some fun in my bedroom." She said he then announced: “My friend will be joining us today,” and alleges Ghislaine Maxwell then entered the room.
Speaking to the Daily Mail Lacerda claimed: “A lot of things happened in that bedroom. They both raped me and this girl. They had sex toys. They did a whole bunch of things.

“I remember the white sheets – everything being white – and just me sitting on my knees, Jeffrey playing with me and the girl, and then Ghislaine walking in and playing with us, and after that, I just blacked out.

“But I know they played with us, and they brought sex toys, and I know that they definitely raped me and this girl.” She added: "I really want to talk to this girl, because a lot of things happened in that bedroom. I know sex toys were used, I know they raped us, but I can't remember the detail."
Maxwell is currently serving a 20-year sentence for sex trafficking minors for Epstein after being convicted in 2021.

At the disgraced British socialites' trial three of her accusers – Jane, Carolyn and Annie Farmer – gave testimony that the sex trafficker touched them. Some of the girls were as young as 14 at the time.
Lacerda also went into horrific detail about how Maxwell allegedly recruited her victims.

She said: “She was going out to different schools and recruiting girls for Jeffrey and then taking advantage of them. She wasn't just a madam getting him girls. She was participating full on in these abuses.”

She added: "Some of the victims say that Maxwell was worse than Jeffrey Epstein, because not only was she an abuser, but she was very mean. She was jealous that Jeffrey liked some of these girls." The 37-year-old revealed Epstein groomed her very slowly and he gained her trust over several months.
She recounted a horrifying story of giving the predator a massage, only to be ordered to take her shirt and bra off. Lacerda added during the massage Epstein exposed himself.

"And then next thing you know, he's raping me. He knew everything about me. The only thing I think Jeffrey did not know was that I got abused by my stepfather, but he knew where I was living, that I had a boyfriend and I was taking care of my sister. He knew all of this.”

"He would use me as a doll, prompt me to say to the girls, 'this is how you're supposed to do things, this is how you're supposed to move your body'. He would say: 'This is what a good girl would do.'" Epstein would later ask to meet Lacerda's sister, who he knew was about 10 or 11 at the time. Despite the pervert begging to meet the child Lacerda refused.

Lacerda, who waved her anonymity in September, said: “I’m going to speak for myself: I will testify under oath in front of the whole world and Congress, again, with no problem – as long as the men that are on the files will testify and there will be criminal charges put against them.”

She added: ‘I'm just not the type of person who's going to live in the past. I have a life today. I have a daughter. I have my four dogs. I have my health. I have the opportunity to help people break their silence and share my story. Why am I going to be sad?
“I love my life. I'm scared of dying. I want to live for a long time. And while I'm here, I want to enjoy it.”

V1_1.title: Epstein raped girl and model in same bed - then ‘asked to meet her 11-year-old sister’
